steps.title: Recognize that exactly one intersection means the line touches the vertex
steps.content: A parabola intersects a horizontal line $y = c$ at exactly one point when the line is tangent to the parabola. This occurs at the vertex of the parabola.

steps.title: Find the x-coordinate of the vertex
steps.content:
For a parabola $y = ax^2 + bx + d$, the vertex occurs at $x = -\frac{b}{2a}$.

Here, $a = -1$ and $b = 9$, so:
$$x = -\frac{9}{2(-1)} = \frac{9}{2}$$

steps.title: Evaluate the parabola at the vertex to find c
steps.content:
Substitute $x = \frac{9}{2}$ into $y = -x^2 + 9x - 100$:
$$c = -\left(\frac{9}{2}\right)^2 + 9 \cdot \frac{9}{2} - 100$$
Computing each term:
$$c = -\frac{81}{4} + \frac{162}{4} - \frac{400}{4}$$
Combining over a common denominator:
$$c = \frac{-81 + 162 - 400}{4} = \frac{-319}{4} = -\frac{319}{4}$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.
